document.title: SCF Bootstrap Foundational Authority
document.authority_scope: Defines the initial generic SCF architecture, authority model, construction constraints, and minimum conformance rules from which later governed specifications may be developed.

field_semantics.interpretation: Descriptive metadata identifies and explains the document but does not independently impose conformance requirements. Normative authority defines required architecture, behavior, constraints, vocabulary, or conformance.

scf.name: Session Continuity Framework
scf.purpose: Define self-contained, model-assisted applications that preserve durable authority across transient sessions while limiting drift, hallucination, uncontrolled inference, and direct mutation of authoritative state.

contract_foundation.name: SCF Contract Foundation
contract_foundation.role: Nested foundational specification defining the generic architecture, authority model, construction constraints, and minimum conformance rules for Session Continuity Framework applications.

dataset.role: Sole authority for committed application information state.
must_not: treat transient chatbot state as committed state

rules.statement: The ruleset is the stable authority for behavior and data-model meaning.
The dataset is the sole authority for committed application information.
The chatbot performs transient semantic execution and owns no committed state.
Transient conversation or model state must never substitute for committed authority.
Only explicit structured payloads may cross a chatbot-to-authority mutation boundary.
The chatbot must not directly mutate authoritative artifacts.
A controlled commit mechanism validates and mechanically applies approved payloads without inventing semantics.
Proposed information remains nonauthoritative until commitment is confirmed.
Partial or failed commitment must not be reported as complete success.

context_model.objective: Provide the smallest authoritative context sufficient for the active task.

rules.statement: Context selection begins with the active task, not recursive repository loading.
Every lower-level task remains traceable to the higher-level authority that constrains it.
Only task-relevant documents, source files, and tests are loaded.
Context identity and revision must be recordable so the input can be reproduced.
Missing required authority makes the task underspecified; it is not permission to infer architecture.

levels.1: Architecture-wide responsibilities, terminology, authority relationships, and context rules.
levels.2: Component contracts, interfaces, schemas, and externally observable behavior.
levels.implementation: Non-normative realization of Level 2 contracts unless authority is explicitly delegated.

rules.statement: Higher specification levels constrain lower levels.
Lower levels must not reinterpret or override higher-level authority.
Every normative document has one declared parent except the Level 0 root.
Specification changes and implementation changes are separate operations.

construction_model.decomposition: Top down from architecture to contracts to bounded implementation tasks.
construction_model.synthesis: Bottom up from small independently testable units.

rules.statement: A generator receives only one bounded synthesis task at a time.
A synthesis task must not expand its own architectural or write scope.
The development pipeline controls implementation context and scope; it is not a fourth SCF component.

foundation_model.contract_foundation: Generic SCF architecture and contracts used to construct self-contained SCF applications.

rules.statement: The Contract Foundation contains no application-specific rules or application data.
A completed SCF application has no continuing runtime dependency on the Contract Foundation.
SCF is independent of model provider, interface, storage medium, programming language, source-control system, and application domain.

controlled_vocabulary.authority: A source whose declarations govern interpretation or valid state within its assigned domain.
controlled_vocabulary.ruleset: Stable behavioral and data-model authority for one SCF application.
controlled_vocabulary.dataset: Durable authoritative application information governed by a ruleset.
controlled_vocabulary.chatbot: Transient model-assisted semantic executor governed by a ruleset.
controlled_vocabulary.structured_payload: Explicit machine-validatable representation of a proposed authoritative change.
controlled_vocabulary.controlled_commit_mechanism: Mechanism that validates and mechanically applies approved structured payloads without inventing semantics.
controlled_vocabulary.session_buffer: Transient, nonauthoritative working information accumulated during an interaction.
controlled_vocabulary.contract_foundation: Generic architecture and contracts used to construct self-contained SCF applications.
controlled_vocabulary.synthesis_task: Small bounded implementation unit with explicit authority, context, scope, requirements, and acceptance criteria.

conformance_minimum: The system preserves the separation between ruleset, dataset, and chatbot.
Committed application information has one identifiable authority.
Transient model state is never treated as committed authority.
Authoritative mutations use explicit structured payloads and controlled commit mechanisms.
Model context is bounded to the active task and traceable authority.
Lower-level specifications and implementations conform to higher-level authority.
Missing authority causes an underspecified result rather than architectural invention.
